sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.17 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group and let$d=d(G)$be the least positive integer such that$G$satisfies a law$[x,\, _ry]=[x,\, _{r+d} y]$for some nonnegative integer$r=r(G)$. [r]{a)} Let$e=1$if$d(G)$is even and$e=2$otherwise. Is it true that the exponent of$G/F(G)$divides$e d(G)$? [r]{b)} If$G$is a nonabelian simple group, does the exponent of$G$divide$d(G)$? Part a) is true for soluble groups (N.\,D.\,Gupta, ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
